us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Senate returns after Lindsey Graham’s sudden death
bullets: Sen. Lindsey Graham died Saturday at age 71 after a sudden illness.
Preliminary findings cited an aortic dissection linked to cardiovascular disease.
Senate Republicans returned to Washington with leadership and agenda questions unresolved.

articles.title: South Carolina faces special-election process to replace Graham
bullets: South Carolina law sets a one-week filing period beginning July 21.
The vacancy follows Graham’s death after decades in Congress.
The race will determine who fills a key Republican Senate seat.

articles.title: Los Angeles County wildfire prompts evacuations
bullets: A fast-moving fire triggered evacuation orders in a remote high-desert area.
The blaze spread Friday amid severe heat and elevated wildfire risk.
Officials warned dry and hot conditions could complicate firefighting.

articles.title: Dangerous heat threatens more than 100 million Americans
bullets: A coast-to-coast heat wave is affecting large parts of the United States.
Salt Lake City and Billings reported preliminary all-time record highs Sunday.
Forecasters warned of dangerous conditions from the West to the East Coast.

articles.title: Cyclosporiasis outbreak spreads across dozens of states
bullets: Health officials are tracking a summer rise in Cyclospora infections.
Axios reported at least 843 U.S. cases across 31 states as of July 9.
Some restaurants temporarily pulled fresh ingredients tied to outbreak concerns.

articles.title: US and Iran trade attacks as Hormuz tensions escalate
bullets: Both the U.S. and Iran asserted control over the Strait of Hormuz.
The latest escalation followed an Iranian attack on a container ship.
Oil and shipping risks rose as diplomacy faced new pressure.

articles.title: Bangkok bar fire kills at least 27 people
bullets: A fire tore through a Bangkok beer hall late Sunday into Monday.
At least 27 people were killed and dozens were injured.
Thai authorities began investigating the cause and possible exit failures.

articles.title: Zelenskyy seeks more European air defenses in Paris
bullets: Ukraine’s president pressed allies for additional air-defense support.
Kyiv reported strikes on Russian vessels in the Sea of Azov from July 6–13.
Russia said it thwarted Ukrainian drone plots against distant air bases.

articles.title: Fontainebleau forest fire forces evacuations near Paris
bullets: A major wildfire burned in the historic Fontainebleau forest south of Paris.
Authorities evacuated residential areas and disrupted transport routes.
The fire came as western Europe faced another red-alert heat wave.

articles.title: Qatar’s former ruler Sheikh Hamad dies at 74
bullets: Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani died Sunday, state media reported.
He ruled Qatar from 1995 to 2013 before transferring power to his son.
He was credited with transforming Qatar into a major diplomatic and media player.

articles.title: Supreme Court declines nationwide porn-viewing ban plea
bullets: The Supreme Court refused to entertain a petition seeking a nationwide ban.
The bench said the issue was important but did not warrant its examination as framed.
The matter involved questions around online content and public policy.

articles.title: India’s June exports rise 15.5%; trade deficit widens
bullets: Merchandise exports rose 15.5% year over year to $40.41 billion in June.
Imports increased about 31% to $70.84 billion.
The trade deficit widened to $30.43 billion.
articles.source: Business Standard / Press Trust of India

articles.title: India declares mourning for Qatar’s former emir
bullets: India declared one day of national mourning for Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani.
The national flag was to fly at half-mast across India on July 13.
The former Qatari ruler died at age 74.

articles.title: HDFC Bank cuts more than 3,300 jobs amid automation push
bullets: HDFC Bank reduced its workforce by 3,343 employees in FY26.
The drop was linked to automation, AI deployment and operational restructuring.
Shares fell early Monday before paring losses.

articles.title: India holds out for better terms in US trade talks
bullets: India rejected a quick interim trade agreement with the United States.
Reuters reported New Delhi wants stronger assurances on tariffs and levies.
Talks continued after USTR Jamieson Greer’s visit failed to finalize a deal.

articles.title: Oil jumps as US-Iran clashes rattle Wall Street
bullets: Crude prices rose after renewed U.S.-Iran attacks raised Hormuz shipping concerns.
U.S. stock indexes traded mixed as investors weighed geopolitical risks.
Energy-market volatility returned after earlier easing in oil prices.

articles.title: Nvidia leads AI-stock strength into the weekend
bullets: Nvidia rose 4% Friday and was the strongest force lifting the S&P 500.
The move showed continued investor appetite for AI-related winners.
Tech strength helped Wall Street tick higher before Monday’s renewed volatility.

articles.title: WD-40 surges after stronger-than-expected profit
bullets: WD-40 shares jumped 10.6% after quarterly profit beat expectations.
The stock was among Friday’s standout individual gainers.
The move came during a broader session supported by AI-related stocks.

articles.title: SK Hynix makes major US trading debut
bullets: SK Hynix American depositary shares were priced at $149 each.
The listing raised roughly $26.5 billion, according to AP’s market report.
The shares finished their first session up 13.1%.

articles.title: AI-chip volatility remains a key market risk
bullets: Recent sessions showed sharp swings in chip and AI-linked shares.
AP noted that large AI stocks now carry outsized weight in major indexes.
Investors are watching whether tech momentum can offset oil and rate pressures.

articles.title: Goldman lawyer Ruemmler to face House questions on Epstein ties
bullets: Goldman Sachs senior counsel Kathryn Ruemmler is set to face congressional scrutiny.
Lawmakers are examining her ties to Jeffrey Epstein.
The issue keeps Goldman in the spotlight over its handling of her role.

articles.title: Iraq’s prime minister seeks US energy investment
bullets: Iraq’s new prime minister plans to seek major U.S. oil, gas and power investments.
The visit follows damage to Iraq’s finances from the Iran war and crude disruptions.
The talks are expected to focus on energy infrastructure and state finances.

articles.title: HDFC Bank posts first workforce drop in nine years
bullets: HDFC Bank cut employee count by 3,343 in FY26.
Business Standard said it was the lender’s first workforce decline in nine years.
The bank cited structured AI use across customer and operational workflows.

articles.title: India’s solar surplus goes unused despite record output
bullets: India’s solar generation has surged to record levels.
Business Standard reported an average 24 GWh went unused daily in May 2026.
Grid absorption limits left surplus power stranded before evening demand.

articles.title: Dubai housing sales fall in first half of 2026
bullets: Dubai residential sales declined 16% in H1 2026, according to Anarock data reported by Business Standard.
Average prices still rose 6% from a year earlier.
The market remained resilient despite West Asia geopolitical tensions.

articles.title: Anthropic extends free Claude Fable 5 access again
bullets: Anthropic extended free Claude Fable 5 access for paid subscribers until July 19.
The extension delays a shift toward pay-per-use access for the model.
Claude Code weekly rate limits remain higher during the promotion.

articles.title: Apple reportedly plans chip roadmap without M6 Pro and M6 Max
bullets: Apple is reportedly preparing a base M6 chip but skipping M6 Pro and M6 Max.
Bloomberg’s Mark Gurman reported Apple will move toward M7 chips in 2027.
An M7 Ultra chip is reportedly planned for 2028.

articles.title: India reviews Meta response in Instagram child-safety case
bullets: India’s IT ministry is examining Meta’s reply over alleged child-abuse ads on Instagram.
The government had issued a notice after reports about paid ads linked to CSEAM.
Officials said action would follow after reviewing Meta’s explanation.

articles.title: Meta pushes paid AI model access for developers
bullets: Meta is preparing to charge developers for access to its Muse Spark AI model.
Axios reported the plan as part of Meta’s effort to turn AI into revenue.
The move targets competition with OpenAI and Anthropic.

articles.title: US lifts restrictions on Anthropic Claude models after cybersecurity alarm
bullets: The Trump administration lifted restrictions on Anthropic’s Claude models last week.
Anthropic said Claude Fable 5 became widely available after the change.
The episode centered on cybersecurity concerns tied to advanced AI capabilities.
